channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump's sentencing still on for Friday after judge rebuffs his push for a delay while he appeals
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump remains on track to be sentenced this week in his hush money case after a judge on Monday denied his request to halt proceedings while he appeals a ruling that upheld the historic verdict. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump Jr. plans Greenland visit after father stirs controversy
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. is planning to head to Greenland for a day-long trip just weeks after his father, President-elect Trump, said U.S. ownership of the Danish territory "is an absolute necessity.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, KREX 5

item.title: U-Haul stolen in Albuquerque with Nine Inch Nails memorabilia inside
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – It was not the trip one man driving through Albuquerque expected: his U-Haul full of priceless items from one of his favorite bands was stolen. Ryan Jonik said he's out more than $50,000 from the items stolen, not to mention the sentimental value.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea says it tested hypersonic intermediate range missile aimed at remote Pacific targets
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Tuesday its latest weapons test was a new hypersonic intermediate-range missile designed to strike remote targets in the Pacific as leader Kim Jong Un vowed to further expand his collection of nuclear-capable weapons to counter rival nations.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden will honor tribal requests by designating 2 new national monuments in California
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is signing a proclamation to establish two new national monuments in California, in part to honor two tribes, a person familiar with the decision said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JAIMIE DING and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Self-help author James Arthur Ray, whose seminar led to tragedy and death, dies at 67
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — James Arthur Ray, a self-help guru whose multimillion-dollar business toppled after he led a sweat lodge ceremony in Arizona that left three people dead, has died. He was 67.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court Monday for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a $148 million defamation judgment granted to two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is HMPV? CDC 'monitoring' reports of spiking cases in China
item.description: The CDC’s actions come in response to China's government-run media confirming that positivity rates of HMPV hare rising in children under the age of 14, despite respiratory illnesses being down overall since last year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's 2024 win, without the Jan. 6 mob violence of four years ago
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump as the winner of the 2024 election in proceedings Monday that unfolded without challenge, in stark contrast to the Jan. 6, 2021, violence as his mob of supporters stormed the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, MARY CLARE JALONICK, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: British prime minister condemns ‘lies and misinformation’ after Musk attacks
item.description: Elon Musk has criticized Keir Starmer, called for new elections in the U.K. and voiced support for far-right figures in the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: The drama-free proceeding stood in stark contrast to four years ago when pro-Trump rioters violently stormed the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: A 3-year-old boy's death in Honolulu fireworks explosion raises toll to 4
item.description: A 3-year-old boy has died of injuries in the massive explosion of fireworks at a home in Honolulu on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star allegedly choked, punched children's mother: police
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by Nexstar's WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, ABC 4

item.title: Zendaya and Tom Holland are engaged: Report
item.description: Speculation of a possible engagement started circulating online after Zendaya was seen at the Golden Globes Sunday wearing a massive diamond on her ring finger.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detachment risk prompts recall of 145K trailer hitch locks
item.description: The recall says the vehicle may become detached from whatever it's towing due to the potentially loose part on the hitch locks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: San Diego bishop, Trump critic moved to Washington Diocese
item.description: The pope’s move put a vocal supporter of migrants at the top post in Washington just weeks before Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Biden issues ban on offshore oil and gas drilling in most federal waters. Trump vows to undo it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is moving to ban new offshore oil and gas drilling in most U.S. coastal waters, a last-minute effort to block possible action by the incoming Trump administration to expand offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione hires Harvey Weinstein’s prison consultant Craig Rothfeld: report
item.description: UnitedHealthcare murder suspect Luigi Mangione has retained Craig Rothfeld, a former white-collar inmate turned prison consultant who also worked for Harvey Weinstein.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: British man and a nursing assistant were the last victims identified in New Orleans attack
item.description: The coroner's office said all the victims died from blunt force injuries. The suspect in the attack, Shamsud-Din Jabbar, was fatally shot in a firefight with police.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Chiefs finally depart KC for Denver amid ice storm after 4 hours spent waiting on the tarmac
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Chiefs finally departed from Kansas City International Airport for their game in Denver on Saturday after spending about four hours stranded on the tarmac amid an ice storm that blanketed the region ahead of an impending blizzard.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 39,000 John Deere mowers recalled
item.description: About 39,000 John Deere ZTrak™ Zero Turn Mowers with Kawasaki Engines have been recalled because the voltage regulator inside the mower's engine can fail while in use or while the product is being stored.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KRON4

item.title: Filmmaker and husband to Aubrey Plaza, Jeff Baena, dies by suicide: report
item.description: Film writer and director Jeff Baena, who is also known for his marriage to Hollywood actress Aubrey Plaza, has died at the age of 47, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Florida mom makes teen son turn himself in for crime
item.description: A 15-year-old boy accused of armed attempted robbery was brought to Florida deputies to be turned in, by his own mother.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans attacker’s apparent loyalty to Islamic State group highlights persistent threat of lone wolf terrorism
item.description: The deadly Jan. 1, 2025, attack in New Orleans serves as a reminder of the persistent threat to the U.S. from individuals inspired by extremist Islamist groups.
item.dc:creator: Sara Harmouch, American University, KRON4

item.title: IRS reminds taxpayers in 24 disaster area states of 2025 deadlines
item.description: A tax filing extension for residents of two dozen states affected by disasters is coming to an end in 2025, the IRS reminded taxpayers Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Fox 5

item.title: Mother, son given lengthy prison terms for roles in the killings of 8 Ohio family members
item.description: Two more family members convicted in the killings of eight members of an Ohio family received lengthy prison terms on Friday for their roles in the 2016 shootings, as prosecutions near completion in what has been described as the most heinous crime in modern Ohio history.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sunday school class with Jimmy Carter: What it was like
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — It never got old. No matter how many times one crammed into the modest sanctuary at Maranatha Baptist Church, there was always some wisdom to be gleaned from the measured, Bible-inspired words of Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France. The State Department announced Friday that Blinken would visit Seoul, Tokyo and Paris beginning Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Soldier in Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion had gunshot wound to head: sheriff
item.description: The man who drove a Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside of a Las Vegas, Nevada hotel has been identified as Matthew Livelsberger, an Army soldier from Colorado, a law enforcement source told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, ABC 4
